title: 1. Client Overview
content:
- **Business name:** Mercury Auto Transport
- **Website:** https://mercuryautotransport.com/
- **Niche:** Auto transport / car shipping
- **Primary SEO scope evidenced in the reports:** National organic SEO plus state/city landing page expansion, with a local visibility component through maps-oriented work.
- **Target areas mentioned:** U.S. national terms, multiple states, multiple cities, with especially heavy Florida-related keyword work and Miami-specific gains called out.
- **Key contacts:** Not identified in the uploaded reports.
- **Social / GBP / GMB links:** Not provided in the uploaded reports.

title: 2. Client Persona & Communication Style
content:
- The uploaded reports do not include direct client communication, so communication style cannot be confirmed from source material alone.
- Based on the reporting format, the account appears to be managed around **performance outcomes**: traffic growth, rankings, indexed pages, SERP features, clicks, and deliverables completed.
- The reporting language suggests the client is likely attentive to:
  - growth in organic traffic
  - movement in commercial keyword rankings
  - local/state page expansion
  - visible deliverables such as backlinks, citations, and landing pages
- No direct sensitivities, preferences, or update-channel preferences are documented in the uploaded files.

title: 3. Business Model / Service Focus
content:
- Mercury Auto Transport operates in the **car shipping / auto transport** space.
- Core keyword themes shown in the reports include:
  - car shipping quotes
  - affordable car shipping companies
  - auto movers near me
  - best car moving company
  - car hauling companies
  - car movers long distance
  - shipping car across country
  - local car transport companies
  - auto transport near me
  - vehicle movers
  - best company to ship car to another state
- This indicates a mix of:
  - national commercial intent terms
  - long-distance / cross-country transport intent
  - “near me” and local-intent transport queries
  - state- and city-level location SEO

title: 4. Work Completed So Far
content:
The June report states the following work was completed or active:


### Content / On-page
- 15 new city- or location-specific landing pages created.
- Each new page reportedly includes:
  - 600+ words of unique SEO content
  - localized H1/H2 structure
  - SEO-optimized meta titles and descriptions
  - internal links
  - mobile/UX-friendly formatting
  - Google Map embeds
  - local weather widgets
- Content was rewritten for 15+ key location pages.
- On-page fixes included:
  - new page creation
  - page title optimization
  - page description optimization
  - adding weather widget and embed code


### Off-page
- 40+ branded citations were secured.
- 7 guest blog posts were published on higher-authority platforms.
- Web 2.0 and contextual backlinks were also built.


### Reporting / Tracking
- Organic traffic reporting
- Search Console reporting
- Google Analytics reporting
- Indexation tracking
- Heatmap / Maps ranking comparison screenshots

title: 5. Reported Results / What Worked
content:
These are the headline outcomes explicitly claimed in the uploaded reports:


### Organic visibility
- Organic traffic increased from **11.5K to 14.7K** (+30.1%).
- Ranking keyword count increased to **12,000+ keywords in the U.S.** (+26.29%).
- Non-branded traffic increased to **10.1K** (+50.42%).
- Branded traffic was reported at **4.6K** and described as stable.


### SERP feature growth
- SERP feature visibility reportedly rose from **850 to 2,970** (+249%).
- Specific feature growth called out:
  - People Also Ask: 2,317 appearances
  - Image Pack: 319
  - Things to Know: 107
  - AI Overview: 150


### Search Console / Analytics
- Clicks increased from **6.02K to 7.07K** (+17.5%).
- Impressions increased from **1.16M to 1.22M** (+5.2%).
- CTR increased from **0.5% to 0.6%**.
- Average position improved from **33.1 to 30.8**.
- New users from organic increased from **5.81K to 6.7K** (+15.3%).
- Organic sessions increased from **8.33K to 9K** (+8.0%).


### Ranking movement
- Top 3 keywords increased from **128 to 149** (+21).
- Position 4–10 keywords increased from **681 to 704** (+23).
- Multiple state and city pages were said to gain traction.
- Florida and Miami were specifically highlighted for page-one movement.


### Indexation
- Indexed pages reportedly increased from roughly **490 to 535** since early May.


### Maps / local visibility
- The separate heatmap report shows sample Google Maps improvements for several commercial transport terms, but the PDF is mostly image-based and does not expose a detailed numeric breakdown in text.
- It does confirm that maps-related ranking improvements were part of the reporting narrative.

title: 6. Important Claims Requiring Caution / Verification
content:
These points appear in the report and should be treated carefully unless independently verified:
- The statement that Mercury Auto Transport is featured as the **top auto transport company in ChatGPT’s 2025 recommendation list** should be verified independently before it is repeated in client-facing strategy or sales material.
- The July plan mentions a strategy involving buying expired high-DA domains, revamping them, and redirecting them. That can carry quality, spam, and risk considerations and should not be executed casually without strong review.
- The heatmap report is primarily screenshots, so precise before/after keyword-level averages are not easily extractable from the text alone.

title: 7. Risks / Watchouts
content:
- Heavy reliance on location page scaling can create quality-control issues if content uniqueness and usefulness are weak.
- Adding weather widgets to SEO pages may increase page weight or clutter without meaningful commercial impact if not implemented cleanly.
- Citation and guest-post volume should be monitored for quality, relevance, and footprint risk.
- Any expired-domain redirect strategy should be reviewed carefully for spam history, topical mismatch, and long-term risk.
- If reporting references AI visibility claims, those should be backed by reproducible prompts, screenshots, and methodology.

title: 8. Likely Current SEO Strategy
content:
Based on the uploaded reports, the active strategy appears to be:
1. Expand state/city landing page coverage at scale.
2. Improve on-page optimization for localized commercial terms.
3. Build branded citations and links to reinforce authority and indexing.
4. Capture broader SERP feature visibility, including AI Overview / PAA inclusion.
5. Improve national and local-intent keyword coverage simultaneously.

title: 9. Open Questions / Missing Context
content:
The uploaded reports do **not** tell us:
- who the main client contact is
- how quickly the client responds
- what the client values most commercially (calls, booked shipments, revenue, lead quality, etc.)
- exact conversion goals
- which pages or services drive the most revenue
- what tools or dashboards the client has access to
- whether there are any internal constraints, dev bottlenecks, or approval processes
- the actual backlinks/citation lists unless the linked sheets are separately opened and reviewed

title: 10. Recommended Operating Notes for Alex
content:
- Treat the uploaded reports as **partial context**, not a complete account history.
- Preserve continuity around:
  - national + state/city SEO expansion
  - Florida / Miami momentum
  - maps visibility improvements
  - content + link-building cadence
- Verify any “AI recommendation” or “top in ChatGPT” claims before using them operationally.
- Ask for or locate:
  - the backlink sheets
  - citation sheets
  - exact list of created location pages
  - primary client contact details
  - conversion / lead tracking data
  - any previous call notes or email threads
- When proposing next steps, prioritize:
  - pages and terms already showing traction
  - conversion-oriented page improvements
  - revenue-linked keyword clusters
  - quality control on scaled content and link work

title: 11. Source Basis
content: This briefing is based only on the two uploaded June 2025 reports. Where the record is thin, that has been stated directly.

title: What Happened
content:
- Mercury filed a Stripe chargeback for ~$3,450/month × 4 months = ~$13,800 total
- Stripe sided with Mercury. OO lost all 4 months of payments in the dispute (November 2025).
- Mercury genuinely messed OO around — bad faith chargeback.
- Do not re-engage.

title: Lesson
content:
- Do not take payments via Stripe credit card subscriptions for large retainers — too easy for clients to chargeback
- Prefer bank transfer (SEPA / wire) for all retainers going forward — much harder to reverse
